source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple sessions in same cwd
notes: KNOWN FAILING (issue #503 — premise CORRECTED 2026-05-27) — RE-RECORDED with CLEAN turns (LM Studio gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored at 32768 ctx; all 3 turns step-finish reason=stop, ZERO _error/UnknownError). This OVERTURNS #503's headline: the daemon DOES now emit a `working` state_transition for the concurrent same-cwd sessions — `new_state:working` count = 2 (one per ses_, reason 'force ready→working on first activity'), NOT 0. #503's '0 working' evidence was a 4096-CONTEXT ARTIFACT: opencode's ~15-21k system prompt overflowed n_ctx 4096, every turn errored with UnknownError, so no real activity was flushed and no working blip could fire. The REAL residual gap is NARROWER and DIFFERENT: each headless `opencode run` turn is a separate short-lived process; the daemon catches the OPENING `working` edge (transcript_activity → working, ~120-220ms before process_exited) but the per-turn CLOSING `ready` edge is deferred/dropped — it only re-fires when the session row is next re-detected. So ses_1's turn-1 closing `ready` is deferred PAST ses_2's entire arc (emitted only at the alpha2 re-drive re-detection), and ses_2's closing `ready` never fires within the recording window. The 7-phase spec assumes each working→ready PAIR closes before the next session is born; the observed ordering does not satisfy this even with infinitely-wide windows (s1_turn1_done ready lands AFTER s2_birth in wall-clock), so widening max_delay_ms alone cannot make it pass — this is an inherent observability limit of concurrent short-lived `opencode run` turns over the ProcessOwnedStore transport, not a recipe flaw. The LOAD-BEARING scenario truth IS observed correctly: TWO distinct ses_… rows keyed to the SAME cwd, each with its OWN `working` transition, no flap/merge/cross-contamination, and session 1 re-driven via --session while session 2 is alive (see events.jsonl). Single-session headless (opencode/basic-turn) still emits a clean working→ready pair because the process lives across the flush. No driver fix is available — start_session is headless-only and the TUI/run_live path cannot launch concurrent same-cwd sessions. The phase lines below stay as the documented TARGET (NOT rebased to the bug); clears when the daemon surfaces the per-turn closing `ready` for concurrent short-lived `opencode run` turns in a shared cwd. MAINTAINER ACTION: #503's title/body should be CORRECTED (working IS emitted; the gap is the deferred closing-ready), not closed as fixed. ORIGINAL RATIONALE: Two concurrent opencode headless `opencode run` chains in the SAME cwd, driven via the driver's start_session step + inline {"session":N} targeting (no tmux/TUI — start_session stays on the headless path, only reset_session/slash force run_live). opencode keys sessions on the SQLite session.id; the directory column is only a liveness gate (watcher.go scanSessions / liveCWDs), so two same-cwd chains are two independent ses_… rows with independent working→ready arcs — daemon_capability: full. The spec anchors the FIRST phase on the first WORKING transition rather than the first ready, because the shared process scanner emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready that never enters working; anchoring on ready would greedily match that placeholder and cascade same_session_as failures (transport-neutral pitfall — applies to opencode's ProcessOwnedStore too). Windows are wide (≤60s) because the dev default is a slow local LM Studio model (~30-45s/turn). Assertions: (1) session 1 does an independent working→ready turn; (2) a DISTINCT ses_… appears (s2_birth carries new_session:true) and does its own working→ready turn; (3) session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is alive (s1_turn2_*, pinned same_session_as s1_turn1_start), proving the per-session binding stayed stable and never flapped/merged across the two same-cwd rows.

text: Session 1 first prompt drives ready → working (anchors the real ses_… session; the pre-session proc-<PID> row never enters working)
Session 1 first turn completes: working → ready
Session 2 starts in the SAME cwd as a DISTINCT ses_… and appears in ready (new_session proves it is not session 1 — two independent SQLite session rows, not one)
Session 2 first prompt drives ready → working — independent of session 1
Session 2 first turn completes: working → ready
Session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is still alive — a working transition on session 1's original ses_… proves the binding stayed stable and did not flap/merge into session 2
Session 1 second turn completes: working → ready
